definitions.example: A person lifting a box of mass 10 kg through a height of 10 m does work on the box.
definitions.definition: Work done by a force on an object is the product of the force applied and the displacement of the object in the direction of the force. It is a scalar quantity.

definitions.example: A bullet has kinetic energy due to its motion.
definitions.definition: The ability to do work. It is a scalar quantity.

definitions.example: A stone kept at a height has potential energy due to its position.
definitions.definition: Energy an object possesses due to its position or configuration. It is a scalar quantity.

definitions.example: A moving car has kinetic energy due to its motion.
definitions.definition: Energy an object possesses due to its motion. It is a scalar quantity.

definitions.example: A rolling ball has both kinetic and potential energy.
definitions.definition: The sum of kinetic energy and potential energy of an object.

definitions.example: A water pump has power due to the rate at which it does work.
definitions.definition: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred. It is a scalar quantity.

definitions.example: 1 Joule is the energy required to raise a 1 kg object by 1 m.
definitions.definition: The SI unit of work, energy, and power.

definitions.example: 1 Watt is the power required to do 1 Joule of work in 1 second.

definitions.definition: Forces for which the work done by them on an object is path-independent.
Forces for which the work done by them on an object is path-dependent.

definitions.example: A rolling ball has a change in kinetic energy equal to the net work done on it.
definitions.definition: The total change in mechanical energy of an object is equal to the net work done on the object.

definitions.example: A car engine has efficiency due to the ratio of output work to input work.
definitions.definition: The ratio of output work to input work.

definitions.example: A spinning top has kinetic energy of rotation.
definitions.definition: The energy an object possesses due to its rotational motion.

definitions.example: A stretched rubber band has potential energy of rotation.
definitions.definition: The energy an object possesses due to its rotational configuration.

definitions.example: A force applied to a lever causes a torque.
definitions.definition: A measure of the rotational force that causes an object to rotate.

definitions.example: A rotating wheel has a moment of inertia due to its mass distribution.
definitions.definition: A measure of an object's resistance to changes in its rotational motion.

definitions.example: A spinning figure skater has angular momentum due to her rotation.
definitions.definition: A measure of an object's tendency to maintain its rotational motion.
